Cobalt Greaves  
 

Lore Item
Slot: LEGS
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 35
STR: +8 DEX: +8 STA: +8
Effect: Invisibility (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 8.0) at Level 45
WT: 7.5 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR
Race: ALL except IKS
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Cobalt Greaves
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2002 at 4:35 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778955117
8 posts
I got mine tonight....wooot! One of our 60 warriors was wandering through KC and heard that VS was up. He sent out a call and the guild assembled in less than 15min. We took him out with 17 players. Our Chanters chain runed the MA and everyone else beat the snot out of him from behind. It took longer than was desirable but he still ended up dead. It took us two tries. I can tell you that he life taps for 1500, and he does it fast. The first time i died so fast my health bar was still mostly red when i was reloading. He droped Cobalt Greaves, wizzy epic staff and a pair of shissar gaunts <spelling?> uber int caster hand item. Well it worked for us, but we have some good chanters, and one hell of a good MA.


Balguril Ghoulchewer
51 Warrior
Una Salus Victus
Prexus

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 16 2002 at 11:02 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) if people refuse to pay what others are asking... the price will go down... if you could get a bunch of friends together in (your servers sales capitol) you could negotiate prices very easily... when the person goes for a pc... or you do... your friends can give a lower pc... if the person demands more go for less or walk off...
RE: how much??
QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2002 at 2:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591782711376
Scholar
45 posts
Prices go down when supply increases, or demand decreases.
Supply increases two ways: 1, the item gets farmed, and more enter the realms. The day or month after an item comes out, everyone wants one, and there are a few dozen to go around. Six months later, you have hundreds. Watch the price go down.
Some better piece of equipment comes out, everyone sells their stuff when they get the new stuff, so even more of the item are now available (fewer players need them).
Prices never go up unless the item changes. Items come out, and drop slowly in price.
Trying to negotiate that with a couple friends saying "boo, that price is too high" just causes a seller to go to one of the thousand other buyers who want to pay for it. If the price is really too high, the seller wont get the price. If it is reasonable, someone will buy it.
▲ Page top
RE: how much??
QuoteReply
# Feb 27 2013 at 8:46 PM Rating: Decent
Seda
Scholar
83 posts
"Prices go down when supply increases, or demand decreases.
Supply increases two ways: 1, the item gets farmed, and more enter the realms. The day or month after an item comes out, everyone wants one, and there are a few dozen to go around. Six months later, you have hundreds. Watch the price go down.
Some better piece of equipment comes out, everyone sells their stuff when they get the new stuff, so even more of the item are now available (fewer players need them).
Prices never go up unless the item changes. Items come out, and drop slowly in price.
Trying to negotiate that with a couple friends saying "boo, that price is too high" just causes a seller to go to one of the thousand other buyers who want to pay for it. If the price is really too high, the seller wont get the price. If it is reasonable, someone will buy it."

There is something missing in this info that occurs in this game, a very important part of the EQ economy. More players= more mobs killed + more days played = plat pumped into the economy out of nowhere (like the Fed "injecting liquidity"). So your statement "Prices never go up unless the item changes. Items come out, and drop slowly in price. " is incorrect. More plat in circulation within the server will devalue each piece of plat over time, creating inflation and even hyper-inflation. Most EQ servers at this point have seen some crazy price increases due to this inflation. Remember when being a millionaire was a big deal? Now, as a general rule, you can't even retire if you don't have a million socked away. Remember 25 cent Cokes and 10 cent candybars? Inflation is constant, both in RL and in EQ's economy. Therefore, prices indeed DO go up on items... even if the items don't change in any fundamental way.

ps: yes, I know the original post is old, but the point is still valid.

Edited, Feb 27th 2013 9:47pm by Seda
▲ Page top
RE: how much??

# Mar 02 2002 at 12:19 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592251064527
110 posts
Cobalt BP for 150K? Hahaha, on Xegony, I've seen the Cobalt BP go for 60K, tops. Greaves and Bracer for around 30k. You may have seen the BP auctioned for 150K, but no one, even the most desperate would pay that much for it. People can auction whatever they want for an item (like the guy trying to sell a PGT for 500pp a few nights ago, or someone trying to sell a Fungi for 135K). But that doesn't mean anyone will pay for it. :)

Bloodfang - Wood Elf Druid

▲ Page top
Have the greaves
QuoteReply
# Sep 23 2001 at 2:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763201520
15 posts
I recently went on a VS raid and was lucky enough to win these greaves. Cast time is around 8 seconds and the invisibility effect has a random rate. There has been times it lasted long and other times it was a shorter period of time. The graphics are just like crafted armor but a darker hue of blue/purple. The best aspect of these greaves is for travelling purposes. The only downside is that the lvl at which you can cast the invis effect is a time when many mobs can see invis..but regardless it has been a most incredible boost in my AC.

Gaussian Greyhawk
52 Champion of Terris-Thule
▲ Page top
Invis proc
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2002 at 5:34 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246384115
351 posts
It's the same as caster's invis spell.
That too is random duration, great fun :P
Invis yourself, roll the dice and run through a dangerous area. Exciting!

I gather there's fixed-duration invis spells now,... at awfully high levels: so everything's afraid of you and wouldn't attack anyway, except the stuff your level, which can all see invis.

Nice.

anyone knows the cast time of invis ? it would greatly help in pog if the PoG's come after you and you can cast it fast enough to logout invis and come back without aggro.

▲ Page top
RE: cast time ?
QuoteReply
# Jun 27 2001 at 1:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757735289
13 posts
If the raiding party cant handle Protectors I recomend everyone go get a few lvls or a few more people cause youll have to deal with em every 30 min at least 1 anyway. This isnt a flame Im just bringing something to your attention. Best way to do it is to have somone time the spawn wait for the first one then start a timer when it dies, then when its about to repop stop pulling. PoGs are usually childsplay as long as they dont catch you by surprise Further if your a warrior you dont hide or camp out, you stand fight and die if things are going bad at a raid Clerics are suppose to camp out your suppose to make sure they live long enough to. I know this doesnt have to Do with Cobalt greeves. More on the topic can somone preferably from Thollux, send in some Pricing Data on these There need to Be some second opinions.
▲ Page top
RE: cast time ?
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2001 at 11:28 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
About 8 seconds.

1. people don't sell Uber loot(It is against most guild's rules to sell Loot that takes 30+ people to get. if you do not intend to use the item you should allow someone that can use them have them) If you can not understand that concept then you don't ever deserve a chance to get them.
2.This webpage is not your personal Want ad. The posts here should be for information for others.

#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 31 2001 at 5:47 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) To the original dumbass person,
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 08 2001 at 8:03 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) What you win in MY guild on a Raid and its not no drop, just like these greaves, you are NOT allowed to trade it away. I am not allowed to trade my Incarnadine BP away, fyi dropped by overking rare, price around 60k, and I think its damn right. If i get my berserker/warlord/Cobalt BP I wont sell it, I will give it to one of the lower level warriors (that means f ex the lvl 53 warrior in my guild with Tree weave) FOR FREE. if you have 2 54+ characters in different guilds on the same server something is wrong, You will get a problem sooner or later because some day these guilds want to take the same VS, the same Talendor, the same Trakanon and they will argue about that who gets what and you will be between the front lines
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 31 2001 at 4:15 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) To bad that warlords and berserkers BP's are no drop huh tard?
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 03 2001 at 3:08 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) He was referring to giving away his Incarnidine BP if he got Cobalt/Warlords/berserkers. Not giving away his Warlords/Berserkers.
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 21 2001 at 4:53 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Get a *****...you`ll be to her knees as much as you are with your guild thing lol
#Anonymous, Posted: May 01 2001 at 12:00 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) What the hell was that supposed to mean?
#Anonymous, Posted: May 23 2001 at 8:42 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) it means he is an *** that has no honor and would ditch his guild over money/items....obviously he doesnt have a very good guild Or he is just a ****
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 06 2001 at 12:50 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) woman=yummy
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 21 2001 at 11:15 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Correction 95% of the poeple sell there OLD loot
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 21 2001 at 11:15 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Umm to whoever posted the 2nd post Shut the **** up. Ive seen a cobalt bp for sell you know why...???? Because someone got the warlords bp. Your just mad because you dont have the money to buy one. Leave the guy alone.
#Anonymous, Posted: May 31 2001 at 9:12 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) HE was not stating that people do not sell these legs he was stating if you are in a guild and you won them on a role it is only fare to the guild that you give them to a lower lvl that can use them. to say that the item is your from winning a role on a GUILD raid then you are wrong. BTW if they ever do sell for 60k buy them... i have never seen them being sold... one beacuse the people on our server are loyal to there guilds and another is this item is so great that if you were raiding this without a guild only the warriors would take this item and then if you get warlord or somthing better than cobalt then you would probally pass them on to someone you know you would not just sell them.
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 28 2001 at 2:10 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) good point. whoever posted the second response is a dumbass.
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 08 2001 at 8:06 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) bad point
#Anonymous, Posted: May 31 2001 at 9:13 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I agree
